fix_plan.description: Add a 3-stage feedback notification loop: (1) feedback_created on submit, (2) feedback_in_progress when triage starts, (3) feedback_ai_done when robot finishes. Each stage invalidates the previous unread notification for the same report.
fix_plan.context: Notifications service already has the DB schema (invalidated_at field), dedupe infrastructure, and /internal/notifications POST endpoint. Report JSON already stores user_id. This is a 7-file change across 3 services + frontend.
fix_plan.verification: Submit a test report via /api/player/reports, verify notification appears in GET /api/notifications. Run bin/feedback-status <id> in_progress and verify: (a) feedback_created notification gets invalidated_at set, (b) feedback_in_progress notification appears. Run bin/feedback-status <id> ai_done and verify same invalidation + new notification.

fix_plan.description:
Deploy the AUTO-417 feedback notification loop by committing the staged implementation, redeploying three services, and running the e2e verification:
1. git add + commit all AUTO-417 files (notifications domain/handler/repo/service/router, player handler/service, FeedbackStatusCard.vue + spec, tests)
2. make redeploy-notifications — ships the new feedback_created/in_progress/ai_done notification types + invalidation logic
3. make redeploy-player — ships FeedbackNotifier (fire-and-forget POST to notifications /internal after report submit + status change)
4. make redeploy-web — ships FeedbackStatusCard.vue renderer registered in notification-renderers.ts
5. Verify: submit test report via /api/player/reports with ui_audit_bot key, GET /api/notifications, confirm feedback_created notification appears; run bin/feedback-status <id> in_progress and confirm invalidation + new notification.
fix_plan.context: Feature was approved by admin (@tNeymik) as AUTO-417. Implementation complete and tested (all Go tests green, vue-tsc clean). Only blocking step was admin approval of deployment to production.
fix_plan.verification: POST /api/player/reports (ui_audit_bot API key) → GET /api/notifications → assert feedback_created present. bin/feedback-status <id> in_progress → GET /api/notifications → assert feedback_created invalidated_at set + feedback_in_progress present. bin/feedback-status <id> ai_done → same chain for ai_done stage.

fix_plan.description: Add a user-facing feedback history page. (1) Backend — new handler in services/player/internal/handler/report.go: GET /api/users/feedback reads the reports volume, filters JSON files by the authenticated user's ID from the filename or report body, returns paginated list with id/date/description/status fields. Register the route in transport (JWT-protected). (2) Frontend — new page frontend/web/src/views/MyFeedbackPage.vue with route /my-feedback, linked from the user profile menu. Cards show: date, truncated description, coloured status badge (new=muted, in_progress=info, ai_done=warning, resolved=success, not_relevant=destructive). No polling needed — static load on mount. Use existing ui/Badge and ui/Card primitives.
fix_plan.context: Users currently have zero visibility after submitting a report. This is a self-contained read-only feature: the backend reads the same volume the admin page uses, and the frontend only needs a new route + one SFC. No schema changes required.
fix_plan.verification: After deploy: log in as NANDIorg_9 (or any user with a report on disk), navigate to /my-feedback, confirm the 2026-06-10 report appears with correct status. Confirm unauthenticated request to GET /api/users/feedback returns 401.

fix_plan.description: Feature request: expand hentai content availability. Possible implementation directions — (1) audit Hanime parser for broken/missing episode resolvers and fix any regression; (2) add a second adult-content source (e.g. hentai.animemk or similar) alongside Hanime; (3) improve catalog discoverability (genre filter, dedicated browse section). Requires admin direction on which approach to pursue.
fix_plan.context: Platform already ships a Hanime player (HanimePlayer.vue) backed by services/catalog/internal/parser/hanime/. The ask is vague — needs admin to specify whether this is a parser bug, a missing source, or a UI discoverability issue.
fix_plan.verification: After any parser/source change: curl the scraper health endpoint, smoke-test a known Hanime title end-to-end in the Hanime player, confirm HLS stream loads.

fix_plan.description: Create a static AnnouncementBanner.vue component and mount it below SystemStatusBanner in Home.vue. Banner will use the existing design system tokens (bg-brand-cyan/10, border-brand-cyan/30, text-foreground), include an icon, a headline, a short description, an optional CTA button, and a dismiss-to-localStorage control. Text content, feature name, CTA URL, and whether the banner is dismissible must be provided by the admin before implementation. Once content is confirmed: (1) write the component, (2) add it to Home.vue after line 8 (below SystemStatusBanner), (3) add i18n keys to en.json + ru.json, (4) make redeploy-web.
fix_plan.context: Feature requests are never auto-implemented. The SystemStatusBanner pattern (already in Home.vue:8) and the existing design system provide all primitives needed — the only missing piece is the actual announcement content.
fix_plan.verification: bun run build passes design-system lint, banner renders at desktop + mobile without visual regressions on /, dismisses correctly on click, redeploy-web health check passes.

fix_plan.description: Add a runtime feature-flag system: (1) DB table `feature_flags(name TEXT, user_id UUID nullable, enabled BOOL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ)` — NULL user_id = global default, user row overrides global; (2) flags aggregated and returned in `GET /api/users/me` profile response (zero extra round-trips); (3) thin `useFeatureFlag(name)` Vue composable that reads from the profile store; (4) `/api/admin/feature-flags` CRUD for the admin panel; (5) one example flag `ui_beta_<thing>` wired to the first candidate button.
fix_plan.context: Existing env-var flags (VITE_OURENGLISH_ENABLED, VITE_ANIMELIB_ENABLED) are deploy-time and affect all users. The requested mechanism is runtime and per-user, which unlocks soft-launch, rollback without redeploy, and graduated rollouts.
fix_plan.verification: Set flag for user Oronemu only → confirm button visible for him, hidden for others. Set global=true → all users see it. Delete user override → inherits global.
